description: Emails page layout with Sending/Receiving tab bar, page title, API drawer button, and More actions button
ui_details: Page title 'Emails' (h1), horizontal tab bar with 'Sending' and 'Receiving' tabs. Active tab has underline indicator. Right side: API drawer icon button + three-dot More actions button. Sending tab links to /emails, Receiving tab links to /emails/receiving.
behavior: Clicking tabs navigates between Sending and Receiving views. Tabs update URL and active state. More actions dropdown shows 'Go to docs' link.
data_model: N/A — layout only

unit.name: renders Emails page with tab bar
unit.description: Page renders with Sending and Receiving tabs
unit.expected_output: Both tabs visible, Sending tab active by default

e2e.name: navigate between Sending and Receiving tabs
steps: Verify Receiving tab is now active
e2e.expected: Tab navigation works, URL updates, active state changes

edge_cases.name: direct URL to receiving tab
edge_cases.expected: Receiving tab is active, page loads correctly

description: Emails Sending tab filter bar: search, date range picker, status filter, API key filter, export button
ui_details: Filter bar below tabs. Left to right: Search input (Shadow DOM, placeholder 'Search...'), Date range button (default 'Last 15 days'), Status dropdown (default 'All Statuses'), API Key combobox (default 'All API Keys'), Export icon button (right-aligned).
behavior: Search filters emails by recipient or subject. Date range opens popover with presets (Today, Yesterday, Last 3/7/15/30 days) plus calendar for custom range. Status filter dropdown with 12 options (Bounced, Canceled, Clicked, Complained, Delivered, Delivery Delayed, Failed, Opened, Scheduled, Sent, Queued, Suppressed). API Key filter is a combobox for filtering by API key used.
data_model: Filters: { search: string, dateRange: { from: Date, to: Date }, status: EmailStatus | null, apiKeyId: string | null }

unit.name: date range picker renders preset options
unit.description: Date range popover shows 6 preset options
unit.expected_output: Popover with Today, Yesterday, Last 3 days, Last 7 days, Last 15 days, Last 30 days

unit.name: status filter renders all 12 statuses
unit.description: Status dropdown shows all possible email statuses
unit.expected_output: 12 status options with colored indicators

e2e.name: filter emails by status
steps: Verify table updates to show only delivered emails
e2e.expected: Table filters to show only emails with 'delivered' status

e2e.name: filter emails by date range preset
e2e.expected: Date range updates, table re-filters, button label changes

edge_cases.name: search with no results
edge_cases.expected: Table shows empty state or 'no results' message

description: Emails Sending tab data table with columns: To, Status, Subject, Sent
ui_details: Data table with 4 columns. To: avatar + email link. Status: colored badge button. Subject: plain text. Sent: relative time with tooltip. Each row has a three-dot 'More actions' button on the right. Clicking email link navigates to /emails/:id.
behavior: Rows are clickable via the email address link, navigating to the email detail page. Status badges show delivery status with color coding. Relative timestamps update dynamically. Row 'More actions' dropdown has 'Share email' option.
data_model: Email list item: { id: string, to: string, status: EmailStatus, subject: string, sent_at: Date }. EmailStatus enum: bounced | canceled | clicked | complained | delivered | delivery_delayed | failed | opened | scheduled | sent | queued | suppressed

unit.name: renders email rows with correct columns
unit.description: Each email row displays To, Status, Subject, Sent columns
unit.expected_output: Table rows with all 4 columns populated

unit.name: formats relative time correctly
unit.description: Sent column shows relative time like 'about 20 hours ago'
unit.expected_output: 'about 20 hours ago' text

e2e.name: click email navigates to detail page
steps: Click on the first email's recipient link
e2e.expected: Navigation to email detail page with correct email data

edge_cases.name: empty email list
steps: Filter to a date range with no emails
edge_cases.expected: Empty state message displayed

description: Email detail page — metadata display and event timeline
ui_details: Page header: 'Email' label + recipient email as h1. Metadata section with key-value pairs: From (email), Subject (text), To (email), ID (UUID with copy button). Email Events section: vertical timeline with status badge + timestamp for each event (e.g., sent Mar 28 4:14 PM → delivered Mar 28 4:14 PM).
behavior: Copy button copies email ID to clipboard. Event timeline shows chronological delivery events. Status badges are interactive (clickable). Header has API drawer button and More actions (Share email).
data_model: Email detail: { id: string, from: string, to: string, subject: string, html: string, text: string, events: Array<{ type: EmailStatus, timestamp: Date }> }

unit.name: renders email metadata fields
unit.description: Shows From, Subject, To, and ID fields
unit.expected_output: All 4 metadata fields displayed with correct values

unit.name: renders event timeline in order
unit.description: Events displayed chronologically with status and timestamp
unit.input: Email with sent and delivered events
unit.expected_output: Timeline shows sent first, then delivered, with formatted timestamps

e2e.name: view email detail page from list
steps: Verify From, Subject, To, ID fields are populated
Verify Email Events timeline is visible
e2e.expected: Email detail page shows all metadata and delivery events

edge_cases.name: copy email ID to clipboard
steps: Click copy button next to email ID
edge_cases.expected: ID copied to clipboard, toast or button state change confirms copy

description: Email detail page — content tabs: Preview, Plain Text, HTML, Insights
ui_details: 4 horizontal tabs below event timeline. Preview (default): rendered email in iframe. Plain Text: plain text content. HTML: raw HTML source. Insights: deliverability report. Copy button next to tabs copies content of active tab.
behavior: Tabs switch content display. Preview shows rendered email. Insights tab shows two sections: 'Needs attention' (issues) and 'Doing great' (passing checks). Each insight is an expandable accordion item. 11 total insight checks covering DMARC, click/open tracking, subdomain usage, link URLs, plain text, body size, no-reply, image hosting, SVG images, YouTube URLs. Report timestamp shown at bottom.
data_model: Insights: Array<{ name: string, status: 'needs_attention' | 'doing_great', details: string }>

unit.name: renders 4 content tabs
unit.description: Preview, Plain Text, HTML, Insights tabs visible
unit.input: Email detail with html and text content
unit.expected_output: 4 tabs rendered, Preview active by default

unit.name: insights categorized correctly
unit.description: Insights split into needs_attention and doing_great groups
unit.expected_output: Two sections with correct grouping

e2e.name: switch between content tabs
steps: Navigate to an email detail page
Verify HTML source code is displayed
e2e.expected: Tab switching works, content updates for each tab

edge_cases.name: expand insight accordion item
steps: Click on 'Include valid DMARC record' in Insights tab
edge_cases.expected: Accordion expands to show detailed explanation

description: API code drawer — slide-in panel with multi-language code examples
ui_details: Right-side slide-in drawer triggered by API drawer icon button. Title matches page context (e.g., 'Sending Email API'). Language tabs: Node.js (default), Ruby, PHP, Python, Go, Rust, Java, .NET, cURL. Multiple code sections (Send Email, Send Batch Emails, Retrieve Email, Update Email). Each section has syntax-highlighted code block with copy button. Close button (X) at top.
behavior: Clicking API drawer button opens panel from right. Language tabs switch all code blocks. Copy button copies code to clipboard. Close button or clicking outside closes drawer. For clone: only show Node.js and cURL tabs (per spec: TypeScript/Node.js only).
data_model: API examples: Array<{ title: string, code: Record<Language, string> }>

unit.name: renders API drawer with code sections
unit.description: Drawer shows correct API endpoints with code examples
unit.expected_output: Drawer with Send Email, Send Batch Emails, Retrieve Email, Update Email sections

e2e.name: open and close API drawer
steps: Verify drawer slides in from right
e2e.expected: Drawer opens and closes smoothly with correct content

edge_cases.name: copy code to clipboard
steps: Click copy button on first code block
edge_cases.expected: Code copied to clipboard, button shows success state

description: Emails Receiving tab — list view with empty state
ui_details: Same tab bar as Sending, Receiving tab active. Filter bar: Search input + Date range picker + Export button (fewer filters than Sending). Empty state: centered message 'No received emails yet' with description about predefined address (<anything>@{subdomain}.resend.app) and link to set up custom domain.
behavior: When no received emails exist, empty state is shown with instructions. Each workspace gets a unique subdomain for inbound email. Links to /domains for custom domain setup and to docs.
data_model: Received email: { id: string, from: string, to: string, subject: string, received_at: Date }

unit.name: renders empty state when no received emails
unit.description: Shows empty state message with instructions
unit.expected_output: Empty state with 'No received emails yet' and setup instructions

e2e.name: view receiving tab empty state
steps: Verify empty state message is displayed
Verify link to domains page exists
e2e.expected: Empty state shows with correct message and links

description: Broadcasts list page — table with Name, Status, Created columns, checkbox selection, filters, pagination
ui_details: Page title 'Broadcasts' + 'Create email' button (right of title) + API drawer button. Filter bar: Search input (Shadow DOM), Status dropdown (All Statuses — Draft, Scheduled, Queued, Sent, Failed), Audiences combobox (All Audiences + segment names), Export button. Data table with checkbox column, Name (link to /broadcasts/:id/editor), Status (text badge), Created (relative time). Row actions: Edit, Duplicate, Clone as template, Remove. Pagination: 40/80/120 items per page.
behavior: Clicking broadcast name navigates to editor. Checkboxes enable bulk selection. Status filter narrows list. Audiences filter narrows by segment. Create email button creates new broadcast and navigates to editor. Row actions open dropdown with 4 options (Edit, Duplicate, Clone as template, Remove — Remove has separator above it).
data_model: Broadcast list item: { id: string, name: string, status: BroadcastStatus, created_at: Date }. BroadcastStatus enum: draft | scheduled | queued | sent | failed

unit.name: renders broadcast rows with correct columns
unit.description: Each broadcast row displays Name, Status, Created columns with checkbox
unit.expected_output: Table rows with all 3 columns populated and checkboxes

unit.name: status filter shows 5 broadcast statuses
unit.description: Status dropdown shows Draft, Scheduled, Queued, Sent, Failed
unit.expected_output: 5 status options plus 'All Statuses' default

e2e.name: navigate to broadcast editor from list
steps: Click on a broadcast name link
e2e.expected: Navigation to broadcast editor page

e2e.name: filter broadcasts by status
steps: Verify table updates to show only draft broadcasts
e2e.expected: Table filters to show only drafts

edge_cases.name: row action — remove broadcast
steps: Click three-dot menu on a broadcast row
edge_cases.expected: Confirmation dialog or broadcast removed from list

description: Broadcast editor — form fields (From, Reply-To, To, When, Subscribe to, Subject, Preview text)
ui_details: Full-page editor layout. Header: back arrow + breadcrumb 'Broadcasts' + editable title + draft badge + Test email button + Review button. Form fields: From (autocomplete input, placeholder 'Acme <acme@example.com>'), Reply-To (toggleable via button), To (contenteditable autocomplete for audiences/segments), When (date/time combobox, toggleable), Subscribe to (topic combobox), Subject (text input), Preview text (text input, maxlength 150, toggleable).
behavior: Title is inline-editable. Reply-To, When, and Preview text fields are hidden by default — toggle buttons show them. From input has autocomplete from verified domains. To input autocompletes audience/segment names. When input accepts date/time for scheduling. Subscribe to selects a topic for unsubscribe management. Auto-saves as draft.
data_model: Broadcast editor: { id: string, name: string, from: string, reply_to: string, to: string (audience/segment), when: Date | null, topic_id: string | null, subject: string, preview_text: string, html: string, status: BroadcastStatus }

unit.name: renders all form fields
unit.description: Editor shows From, To, Subject fields by default; Reply-To, When, Preview text are toggleable
unit.expected_output: From, To, Subject visible; Reply-To, When, Preview text hidden until toggled

unit.name: preview text has maxlength 150
unit.description: Preview text input enforces 150 character limit
unit.expected_output: Input truncated or blocked at 150 characters

e2e.name: edit broadcast title inline
e2e.expected: Title changes to 'My Newsletter'

edge_cases.name: test email validation — empty fields
steps: Click 'Test email' button without filling From or Content
edge_cases.expected: Validation message: 'Fill out the From and Content fields before sending a test'

description: Broadcast editor — block-based content editor with slash commands
ui_details: Contenteditable rich text editor with '/' slash command menu. Slash menu categories: Text (Text, Title, Subtitle, Heading, Bullet list, Numbered list, Quote, Code block), Media (Image, YouTube, X/Twitter), Layout (Button, Divider, Section, 2/3/4 columns, Social Links, Unsubscribe Footer), Utility (HTML, Variable). Bottom toolbar tabs: Text (formatting), Image, Components (same as Layout slash commands + HTML + Code), Variables (contact properties + system variables).
behavior: Typing '/' opens command palette with categorized block types. Selecting a block inserts it at cursor. Text toolbar shows Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, Code, Uppercase toggles + alignment (left/center/right) + lists. Variables tab shows insertable template variables: {{{contact.first_name}}}, {{{contact.last_name}}}, {{{contact.email}}}, {{{contact.company_name}}}, {{{RESEND_UNSUBSCRIBE_URL}}}. 'Pick a template' button loads saved templates. 'Upload HTML' accepts .html files.
data_model: Editor blocks: Array<{ type: 'text'|'title'|'subtitle'|'heading'|'bullet_list'|'numbered_list'|'quote'|'code_block'|'image'|'youtube'|'twitter'|'button'|'divider'|'section'|'columns'|'social_links'|'unsubscribe_footer'|'html'|'variable', content: any }>

unit.name: slash command menu shows all block types
unit.description: Typing '/' shows categorized menu with Text, Media, Layout, Utility sections
unit.expected_output: Menu with 4 categories and all block type options

unit.name: variables tab shows contact properties and system variables
unit.description: Variables panel lists 4 contact properties + 1 system variable
unit.expected_output: Shows {{{contact.first_name}}}, {{{contact.last_name}}}, {{{contact.email}}}, {{{contact.company_name}}}, {{{RESEND_UNSUBSCRIBE_URL}}}

e2e.name: insert text block via slash command
e2e.expected: Heading block inserted with 'Hello World' text

edge_cases.name: insert variable into content
edge_cases.expected: Variable placeholder inserted into editor content

description: Broadcast editor — right sidebar: Page style, Theme, Global CSS panels
ui_details: Right sidebar with 3 collapsible panels. Page style: Background (color picker, padding), Body (3 layout options, color, width default 600px, height, padding, rounded corners, border width + color). Theme: 2 presets (minimal, basic), text styles for 4 levels — Text (14px Regular), Title (31px Semi Bold), Subtitle (25px Semi Bold), Heading (19px Semi Bold) — each with color, size, weight, height, spacing, decoration. Global CSS: code editor with quick-insert buttons (@media dark, @media mobile, .button).
behavior: Page style controls affect the email container. Theme presets change all typography at once. Individual text level overrides persist. Global CSS editor allows custom styles with syntax highlighting. Quick-insert buttons add media query or class scaffolding. 'Edit theme' and 'Global CSS' buttons toggle between sidebar panels. Close sidebar button available.
data_model: Broadcast style: { background: { color: string, padding: number }, body: { layout: 'full'|'centered'|'narrow', color: string, width: number, height: number|'auto', padding: number, borderRadius: number, borderWidth: number, borderColor: string }, theme: 'minimal'|'basic', textStyles: Record<'text'|'title'|'subtitle'|'heading', { color: string, fontSize: number, fontWeight: string, lineHeight: number, letterSpacing: number, decoration: 'none'|'underline'|'strikethrough' }>, globalCSS: string }

unit.name: renders page style panel with defaults
unit.description: Body width defaults to 600px, padding to 0, border color to #000000
unit.expected_output: Width=600, padding=0, border color=#000000

e2e.name: switch theme preset
e2e.expected: Theme preset changes, typography updates accordingly

edge_cases.name: custom global CSS
steps: Type 'p { color: red; }' in CSS editor
edge_cases.expected: CSS applied to email preview

description: Broadcast editor — Review panel with slide-to-send
ui_details: Bottom panel triggered by 'Review' button. Shows 'Ready to send?' heading, broadcast details summary (loading state observed), and a 'Slide to send' slider control (range 0-100). Slider requires dragging to confirm send — prevents accidental sends.
behavior: Review button toggles bottom panel. Panel shows broadcast summary (recipients, subject, etc.). Slide-to-send is a slider (aria-label 'Volume', min 0, max 100) — user must drag to 100 to confirm sending. This is a safety mechanism for broadcast sends.
data_model: N/A — interaction only

unit.name: review panel shows slide-to-send slider
unit.description: Panel contains slider with min 0 and max 100
unit.expected_output: Bottom panel with 'Ready to send?' and slider control

e2e.name: open and close review panel
steps: Verify bottom panel appears with 'Ready to send?'
e2e.expected: Review panel toggles open/closed

description: Templates list page — card grid with name, alias, preview thumbnail, status filter, search, create button
ui_details: Page title 'Templates' + 'Create template' button (right of title) + API drawer button. Filter bar: Search input (Shadow DOM), Status combobox (All Statuses, Draft, Published). Card grid layout (not table): each card has preview image thumbnail, template name (link to /templates/:id/editor), alias in monospace (e.g., 'untitled-template'). Card actions button (three-dot menu).
behavior: Clicking card navigates to template editor. Create template button creates new template and navigates to editor. Status filter narrows by draft/published. Card actions dropdown: View details, Edit template, Rename template, Duplicate template, (separator), Remove template.
data_model: Template list item: { id: string, name: string, alias: string, status: TemplateStatus, preview_url: string, created_at: Date }. TemplateStatus enum: draft | published

unit.name: renders template cards with name and alias
unit.description: Each template card displays preview image, name, and alias
unit.expected_output: Card grid with all templates showing name and alias

unit.name: status filter shows Draft and Published options
unit.description: Status combobox shows All Statuses, Draft, Published
unit.expected_output: 3 options: All Statuses, Draft, Published

e2e.name: navigate to template editor from card
e2e.expected: Navigation to template editor page

e2e.name: create new template
e2e.expected: New template created and editor opens

edge_cases.name: card action — remove template
steps: Click three-dot menu on a template card
edge_cases.expected: Confirmation dialog or template removed from grid

description: Template editor — form fields (From, Reply-To, Subject, Preview text) and shared block editor
ui_details: Full-page editor layout. Header: back arrow + breadcrumb 'Templates' + editable title + 'Publish' button (disabled until ready) + More actions button. Form fields: From (autocomplete input), Reply-To (toggleable), Subject (text input), Preview text (toggleable, maxlength 150). NO To, When, or Subscribe to fields. Same block-based content editor as broadcasts with slash commands and toolbar tabs (Text, Image, Components, Variables).
behavior: Title is inline-editable. Reply-To and Preview text hidden by default — toggle buttons show them. Publish button publishes the template (vs Review/Send for broadcasts). More actions: Test email, Version history, View details, Duplicate, Delete. Same content editor as broadcasts with same slash commands, toolbar, and right sidebar (Page style, Theme, Global CSS).
data_model: Template editor: { id: string, name: string, alias: string, from: string, reply_to: string, subject: string, preview_text: string, html: string, status: TemplateStatus }

unit.name: renders template editor form fields
unit.description: Editor shows From and Subject fields; Reply-To and Preview text are toggleable
unit.expected_output: From and Subject visible; Reply-To and Preview text hidden until toggled

unit.name: no recipient or scheduling fields
unit.description: Template editor does not have To, When, or Subscribe to fields
unit.expected_output: No To, When, or Subscribe to fields present

e2e.name: edit template title inline
e2e.expected: Title changes to 'My Template'

edge_cases.name: preview text maxlength enforcement
edge_cases.expected: Input blocked or truncated at 150 characters

description: Template detail page — metadata display with status, variables, alias, creator, and content preview tabs
ui_details: Page header: 'Template' label + template name as h1. Metadata section: Status (badge: draft/published), Variables (count or 'No variables'), Alias (slug with copy button), Created (relative time), Creator (email). Content tabs: Preview (default, iframe), HTML, Plain Text. Copy button next to tabs. API drawer button + More actions button.
behavior: Copy button copies alias to clipboard. Tabs switch between rendered preview, HTML source, and plain text. More actions: Edit template, Rename template, Duplicate template, (separator), Remove template. No Insights tab (unlike email detail).
data_model: Template detail: { id: string, name: string, alias: string, status: TemplateStatus, variables: Array<{ name: string, type: string }>, from: string, subject: string, html: string, text: string, created_at: Date, creator: string }

unit.name: renders template metadata fields
unit.description: Shows Status, Variables, Alias, Created, Creator fields
unit.expected_output: All 5 metadata fields displayed with correct values

Verify detail page loads with metadata
Verify Preview tab is active with rendered content
e2e.expected: Template detail page shows metadata and content preview

e2e.expected: Tab switching works, content updates for each tab

edge_cases.expected: Alias copied to clipboard, button shows success state

description: Audience page layout with 4-tab bar (Contacts, Properties, Segments, Topics), summary stats, and Add contacts dropdown
ui_details: Page title 'Audience' (h1), horizontal tab bar with Contacts (default active), Properties, Segments, Topics tabs. Contacts tab URL: /audience/, Properties: /audience/properties, Segments: /audience/segments, Topics: /audience/topics. Summary stats row: All Contacts (count), Subscribers (count), Unsubscribers (count), Metrics (expandable). 'Add contacts' dropdown button with 'Add manually' and 'Import CSV' options. API drawer button.
behavior: Tab navigation updates URL and active state. Summary stats show real-time counts. Add contacts dropdown opens menu with 2 options.
data_model: N/A — layout only

unit.name: renders Audience page with 4 tabs
unit.description: Page renders with Contacts, Properties, Segments, Topics tabs
unit.expected_output: All 4 tabs visible, Contacts tab active by default

description: Contacts list table with Email, Segments, Status, Added columns, search, filters, and pagination
ui_details: Filter bar: Search input (Shadow DOM, placeholder 'Search by name, email, or multiple emails...'), 'All contacts' dropdown (filters by segment), 'All subscriptions' dropdown (filters by status), Export button. Data table with checkbox column, Email (avatar + email + name link to /audience/contacts/:id), Segments, Status ('Subscribed' green badge), Added (relative time). Row actions on hover.
behavior: Search filters contacts by name/email. Segment filter narrows by segment. Subscription filter narrows by status. Clicking email navigates to contact detail. Checkboxes enable bulk selection. Row actions appear on hover.
data_model: Contact list item: { id: string, email: string, first_name: string, last_name: string, segments: string[], status: 'subscribed' | 'unsubscribed', created_at: Date }

unit.expected_output: Table rows with all 4 columns populated and checkboxes

description: Add contact manually — modal with email addresses textarea and segment autocomplete
ui_details: Modal triggered by 'Add contacts' → 'Add manually'. Title: 'Add contacts'. Email addresses: textarea (placeholder 'foo@gmail.com, bar@gmail.com'), helper text 'Use commas or line breaks to separate multiple email addresses.' Segments: autocomplete contenteditable input. Add + Cancel buttons + close (X).
behavior: Textarea accepts comma or line-break separated emails. Segments autocomplete filters existing segments. Add button creates contacts and assigns them to selected segments. Cancel/Escape closes modal.
data_model: Create contact: { emails: string[], segment_ids: string[] }

unit.name: renders add contact modal with fields

description: Contact detail page — metadata, properties, activity timeline
ui_details: Page header: 'Contact' label + email as h1. Metadata: Email Address, Created (relative time), Status (Subscribed badge), ID (UUID with copy button), Segments (list or 'No segments'), Topics (list or 'No topics'). Properties section: key-value pairs for First Name, Last Name, company_name. Activity section: vertical timeline of events (e.g., 'Contact created'). Note text: 'Activity data may take a few seconds to update.'
behavior: Copy button copies contact ID to clipboard. Properties show all custom contact properties. Activity timeline shows chronological events. More actions dropdown: Edit contact, (separator), Delete contact.
data_model: Contact detail: { id: string, email: string, first_name: string, last_name: string, status: string, segments: Array<{id: string, name: string}>, topics: Array<{id: string, name: string}>, properties: Record<string, string>, created_at: Date, activity: Array<{type: string, timestamp: Date}> }

unit.expected_output: All metadata fields displayed with correct values

unit.expected_output: Properties section with 3 key-value pairs

description: Properties list table with Name, Type, Fallback value, Created columns and Add property modal
ui_details: Filter bar: Search input (placeholder 'Search...'), Type filter combobox ('All Types'). Data table: checkbox column, Name (monospace), Type (string/number), Fallback value, Created (relative time). Row actions. 'Add property' button opens modal with: Name (text, maxlength 100, required, placeholder 'e.g., company_name'), Type (combobox: String, Number), Fallback Value (optional, placeholder 'Value to use when property is empty'). Add + Cancel buttons.
behavior: Search filters properties by name. Type filter narrows by string/number. Add property creates a new custom contact property. Properties appear in contact detail pages.
data_model: Contact property: { id: string, name: string, type: 'string' | 'number', fallback_value: string | null, created_at: Date }

unit.expected_output: 2 type options: String, Number

description: Segments list table with Name, Contacts, Unsubscribed, Created columns and Create segment modal
ui_details: Filter bar: Search input (placeholder 'Search...'). Data table: checkbox column, Name (link to /audience?segmentId=:id), Contacts (count), Unsubscribed (count), Created (relative time). Row actions. 'Create segment' button opens modal with: Name (text, placeholder 'Your segment name'). Add + Cancel buttons.
behavior: Search filters segments by name. Clicking segment name navigates to Contacts tab filtered by that segment. Create segment opens simple name-only modal.
data_model: Segment: { id: string, name: string, contacts_count: number, unsubscribed_count: number, created_at: Date }

e2e.expected: New segment created and visible in table

e2e.name: click segment navigates to filtered contacts
steps: Verify Contacts tab shows filtered view
e2e.expected: Contacts tab loads with segment filter applied

description: Topics list page with empty state, Create topic modal, and unsubscribe page preview
ui_details: Filter bar: Search input (placeholder 'Search...'), Default filter combobox ('Any Default' — opt-in/opt-out). 'Create topic' button + 'Edit Unsubscribe Page' link. Empty state: 'No topics yet' + description + 'Create topic' button + 'Customize page' link. Unsubscribe page preview embedded in page. Create topic modal: Name (maxlength 50, required, placeholder 'Public display name'), Description (textarea, maxlength 200, placeholder 'Optional public description'), Defaults to (Opt-in/Opt-out combobox — immutable after creation), Visibility (Private/Public combobox).
behavior: Topics control unsubscribe preferences. Create topic modal has 4 fields. Defaults to value cannot be changed after creation. Unsubscribe page preview shows live preview of customizable page. Edit Unsubscribe Page link navigates to /audience/topics/unsubscribe-page/edit.
data_model: Topic: { id: string, name: string, description: string | null, default_subscription: 'opt_in' | 'opt_out', visibility: 'private' | 'public', created_at: Date }

unit.name: renders empty state when no topics
unit.description: Shows 'No topics yet' message with create button and unsubscribe preview
unit.expected_output: Empty state with message, create button, customize link, and unsubscribe page preview

unit.name: create topic modal shows all 4 fields
unit.description: Modal has Name, Description, Defaults to, Visibility fields
unit.expected_output: Modal with 4 fields and correct defaults

e2e.name: create new topic
e2e.expected: New topic created with correct settings

edge_cases.name: topic name maxlength
edge_cases.expected: Input truncated or blocked at 50 characters

description: Domains list page layout with filters, data table, and pagination
ui_details: Page title 'Domains' (h1), 'Add domain' button, API drawer button. Filter bar: Search (Shadow DOM input), Status combobox (All Statuses, Pending, Verified, Failed, Not Started), Region combobox (All Regions, North Virginia/us-east-1, Ireland/eu-west-1, São Paulo/sa-east-1, Tokyo/ap-northeast-1), Export button. Data table columns: Domain, Status, Region, Created. Checkbox column for bulk selection. Domain column shows domain name as clickable link to /domains/:id. Status column shows colored badge (verified/pending/failed). Region shows display name + code. Created shows relative time. Pagination: 40/80/120 items per page.
behavior: Status filter narrows list by domain verification state. Region filter narrows by SES region. Search filters by domain name. Clicking domain name navigates to detail page. Row actions: Delete domain only.
data_model: Domain: id, name, status (pending|verified|failed|not_started), region (us-east-1|eu-west-1|sa-east-1|ap-northeast-1), created_at, provider

unit.name: domain status badge rendering
unit.description: Status badge shows correct color for each domain status
unit.expected_output: Badge renders with appropriate color and text

unit.name: region display formatting
unit.description: Region displays both friendly name and code
unit.expected_output: Displays 'North Virginia us-east-1'

e2e.name: filter domains by status
e2e.expected: Table shows only domains with verified status

e2e.name: navigate to domain detail
e2e.expected: Navigates to /domains/:id with domain detail page

edge_cases.name: empty domains list
steps: Filter to a status with no matching domains
edge_cases.expected: Empty state shown with appropriate message

description: Domain detail page with metadata, domain events timeline, and two content tabs (Records, Configuration)
ui_details: 'Domain' breadcrumb + domain name as h1 heading + API drawer button + More actions button. Metadata key-value pairs: Created (relative time), Status (badge), Provider (link, e.g. Cloudflare), Region (name + code). Domain Events timeline: vertical list of events (Domain added, DNS verified, Domain verified) with timestamps. More actions: Restart, Go to docs, Delete domain. Two tabs: Records (default) and Configuration.
behavior: Metadata displays domain info. Timeline shows verification progress. More actions Restart re-triggers verification. Provider is auto-detected and shown as a link. Tabs switch between Records and Configuration views.
data_model: Domain detail extends Domain with: provider (string), events[] (type, timestamp), dns_records[], configuration (click_tracking, open_tracking, tls)

unit.name: domain events timeline rendering
unit.description: Events render in chronological order with timestamps
unit.expected_output: Three timeline items rendered in order with formatted timestamps

unit.name: domain metadata display
unit.description: All metadata fields render correctly
unit.input: domain with status, provider, region, created_at
unit.expected_output: Status badge, provider link, region name+code, relative time all displayed

e2e.name: view domain detail from list
e2e.expected: Domain detail page shows all metadata and events timeline

e2e.name: switch between Records and Configuration tabs
e2e.expected: Tab content switches correctly between Records and Configuration

edge_cases.name: domain with pending status
steps: Navigate to a domain that is still pending verification
edge_cases.expected: Status shows 'pending', timeline shows only 'Domain added' event

description: Domain DNS Records tab — DKIM, SPF, MX records with auto-configure and sending/receiving toggles
ui_details: Records tab content: 'Auto configure' button (form with hidden domainId), Tutorial button, Forward instructions button. Three DNS sections: (1) Domain Verification (DKIM) with link, table (Type, Name, Content, TTL, Priority, Status), TXT record for DKIM key; (2) Enable Sending with SPF toggle switch, MX record (feedback-smtp.us-east-1.amazonses.com, priority 10), TXT record (v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all); (3) Enable Receiving with toggle switch. Long values truncated with [...] expandable tooltip. TTL shows 'Auto'. Status column shows verified/pending badges.
behavior: Auto configure button triggers DNS auto-setup via detected provider API (e.g., Cloudflare). Sending toggle enables/disables sending capability with SPF/MX records. Receiving toggle enables/disables inbound email with additional MX records. Record values are copy-to-clipboard on click. Tutorial shows step-by-step DNS setup guide.
data_model: DomainDNSRecord: type (TXT|MX|CNAME), name (string), content (string), ttl (string|'Auto'), priority (number|null), status (verified|pending|failed). Domain has sending_enabled (boolean), receiving_enabled (boolean).

unit.name: DNS record table rendering
unit.description: Records render with correct columns and truncation
unit.input: Array of DNS records with type, name, content, ttl, priority, status
unit.expected_output: Table renders all columns, long content truncated with [...] indicator

unit.name: sending/receiving toggle state
unit.description: Toggle switches reflect domain capabilities
unit.expected_output: Sending toggle is on (checked), Receiving toggle is off (unchecked)

e2e.name: view DNS records for verified domain
steps: Verify DKIM section shows TXT record with 'verified' status
Verify SPF section shows MX and TXT records
e2e.expected: All DNS records display with correct types, names, and verification statuses

edge_cases.name: domain with unverified records
steps: View a domain where DNS records haven't been configured yet
edge_cases.expected: Status badges show 'pending' or 'not_started', auto-configure button prominent

description: Domain Configuration tab — click tracking, open tracking, and TLS settings
ui_details: Configuration tab content: 3 settings. (1) Click Tracking — toggle switch (default off), description about link modification and redirect; (2) Open Tracking — toggle switch (default off), labeled 'Not Recommended', description about 1x1 pixel GIF tracking with link 'if open tracking is right for you'; (3) TLS — combobox with 2 options: Opportunistic (default, attempts secure then falls back) and Enforced (requires TLS).
behavior: Click Tracking toggle enables/disables link tracking for all emails sent from this domain. Open Tracking toggle enables/disables open pixel — marked not recommended due to deliverability impact. TLS combobox changes encryption policy. All settings persist on change (auto-save, no submit button).
data_model: DomainConfiguration: click_tracking (boolean, default false), open_tracking (boolean, default false), tls ('opportunistic'|'enforced', default 'opportunistic')

unit.name: TLS option values
unit.description: TLS combobox has exactly 2 options
unit.expected_output: Options: ['Opportunistic', 'Enforced']

e2e.name: toggle click tracking
e2e.expected: Click Tracking switch remains on after reload (persisted)

edge_cases.name: toggle open tracking shows warning
edge_cases.expected: Setting is enabled, 'Not Recommended' label still visible
